= AsciiDoc Syntax Quick Reference (KBMemo fixture)
Author Name <author@example.org>
v1.0, 2026-05-25
:toc:
:experimental:
:source-highlighter: highlightjs
:stem: latexmath
:imagesdir: ./assets
:allow-uri-read:
:homepage: https://example.org

// Canonical fixture derived from the AsciiDoc Syntax Quick Reference.
// https://docs.asciidoctor.org/asciidoc/latest/syntax-quick-reference/
//
// Section markers use `// kbmemo:syntax-ref:<id>` for coverage tracking in tests and roadmap.

// kbmemo:syntax-ref:document-header
This fixture exercises AsciiDoc constructs for package tests and manual editor verification.

// === Paragraphs ===

// kbmemo:syntax-ref:paragraphs
Paragraphs don't require special markup in AsciiDoc.
A paragraph is defined by one or more consecutive lines of text.
Line breaks within a paragraph are not displayed.

Leave at least one empty line to begin a new paragraph.

// kbmemo:syntax-ref:paragraphs-literal
A normal paragraph.

 A literal paragraph.
 One or more consecutive lines indented by at least one space.

 The text is shown in a fixed-width (typically monospace) font.
 The lines are preformatted (i.e., as formatted in the source).
 Spaces and newlines,
 like the ones in this sentence,
 are preserved.

// kbmemo:syntax-ref:paragraphs-hardbreaks
Roses are red, +
violets are blue.

[%hardbreaks]
A ruby is red.
Java is black.

// kbmemo:syntax-ref:paragraphs-lead
[.lead]
This text will be styled as a lead paragraph (i.e., larger font).

This paragraph will not be.

// === Text formatting ===

// kbmemo:syntax-ref:text-formatting-constrained
It has *strong* significance to me.

I _cannot_ stress this enough.

Type `OK` to accept.

That *_really_* has to go.

Can't pick one? Let's use them `*_all_*`.

// kbmemo:syntax-ref:text-formatting-unconstrained
**C**reate, **R**ead, **U**pdate, and **D**elete (CRUD)

That's fan__freakin__tastic!

Don't pass generic ``Object``s to methods that accept ``String``s!

It was Beatle**__mania__**!

// kbmemo:syntax-ref:text-formatting-highlight
Mark my words, #automation is essential#.

##Mark##up refers to text that contains formatting ##mark##s.

Where did all the [.underline]#cores# go?

We need [.line-through]#ten# twenty VMs.

A [.myrole]#custom role# must be fulfilled by the theme.

// kbmemo:syntax-ref:text-formatting-super-sub
^super^script

~sub~script

// kbmemo:syntax-ref:text-formatting-smart-quotes
"`double curved quotes`"

'`single curved quotes`'

Olaf's desk was a mess.

A ``std::vector``'s size is the number of items it contains.

All of the werewolves`' desks were a mess.

Olaf had been with the company since the `'00s.

// === Links ===

// kbmemo:syntax-ref:links-autolink
https://asciidoctor.org - automatic!

https://asciidoctor.org[Asciidoctor]

devel@discuss.example.org

mailto:devel@discuss.example.org[Discuss]

mailto:join@discuss.example.org[Subscribe,Subscribe me,I want to join!]

// kbmemo:syntax-ref:links-attributes
https://chat.asciidoc.org[Discuss AsciiDoc,role=external,window=_blank]

https://chat.asciidoc.org[Discuss AsciiDoc^]

// kbmemo:syntax-ref:links-special-chars
link:++https://example.org/?q=[a b]++[URL with special characters]

https://example.org/?q=%5Ba%20b%5D[URL with special characters]

// kbmemo:syntax-ref:links-relative
link:index.html[Docs]

// kbmemo:syntax-ref:links-unc
link:\\server\share\whitepaper.pdf[Whitepaper]

// kbmemo:syntax-ref:links-anchors
[[bookmark-a]]Inline anchors make arbitrary content referenceable.

[#bookmark-b]#Inline anchors can be applied to a phrase like this one.#

anchor:bookmark-c[]Use a cross reference to link to this location.

[[bookmark-d,last paragraph]]The xreflabel attribute will be used as link text in the cross-reference link.

// kbmemo:syntax-ref:links-xref
See <<paragraphs>> to learn how to write paragraphs.

Learn how to organize the document into <<section-titles,sections>>.

// kbmemo:syntax-ref:links-interdoc
Refer to xref:other-document.adoc#section-b[Section B of Document B] for more information.

If you never return from xref:other-document.adoc[Document B], we'll send help.

// === Section titles ===

// kbmemo:syntax-ref:sections-article
== Level 1 Section Title

=== Level 2 Section Title

==== Level 3 Section Title

===== Level 4 Section Title

====== Level 5 Section Title

== Another Level 1 Section Title

// kbmemo:syntax-ref:sections-discrete
[discrete]
=== I'm an independent heading!

This paragraph is its sibling, not its child.

// kbmemo:syntax-ref:sections-book
[source,asciidoc]
----
= Level 0 Section Title (Part)

== Level 1 Section Title in Part

= Another Level 0 Section Title (Part)
----

// `:doctype: book` が必要。上は listing 内の参照用サンプル。

// === Includes ===

// kbmemo:syntax-ref:includes
include::basics.adoc[]

include::installation.adoc[]

include::filename.txt[tag=definition]

include::filename.txt[lines=5..10]

include::https://raw.githubusercontent.com/asciidoctor/asciidoctor/main/README.adoc[]

// === Lists ===

// kbmemo:syntax-ref:lists-unordered
* List item
** Nested list item
*** Deeper nested list item
* List item
 ** Another nested list item
* List item

// kbmemo:syntax-ref:lists-ordered
. Step 1
. Step 2
.. Step 2a
.. Step 2b
. Step 3

// kbmemo:syntax-ref:lists-checklist
* [*] checked
* [x] also checked
* [ ] not checked
* normal list item

// kbmemo:syntax-ref:lists-description
First term:: The description can be placed on the same line
as the term.
Second term::
Description of the second term.
The description can also start on its own line.

// kbmemo:syntax-ref:lists-qanda
[qanda]
What is the answer?::
This is the answer.

Are cameras allowed?::
Are backpacks allowed?::
No.

// kbmemo:syntax-ref:lists-mixed
Operating Systems::
  Linux:::
    . Fedora
      * Desktop
    . Ubuntu
      * Desktop
      * Server
  BSD:::
    . FreeBSD
    . NetBSD

Cloud Providers::
  PaaS:::
    . OpenShift
    . CloudBees
  IaaS:::
    . Amazon EC2
    . Rackspace

// kbmemo:syntax-ref:lists-complex
* Every list item has at least one paragraph of content,
  which may be wrapped, even using a hanging indent.
+
Additional paragraphs or blocks are adjoined by putting
a list continuation on a line adjacent to both blocks.
+
list continuation:: a plus sign (`+`) on a line by itself

* A literal paragraph does not require a list continuation.

 $ cd projects/my-book

* AsciiDoc lists may contain any compound content.
+
|===
|Column 1, Header Row |Column 2, Header Row

|Column 1, Row 1
|Column 2, Row 1
|===

// === Images ===

// kbmemo:syntax-ref:images-block
image::sunset.jpg[]

image::sunset.jpg[Sunset]

.A mountain sunset
[#img-sunset,caption="Figure 1: ",link=https://www.example.com/photos/sunset]
image::sunset.jpg[Sunset,200,100]

image::https://asciidoctor.org/images/octocat.jpg[GitHub mascot]

// kbmemo:syntax-ref:images-inline
Click image:play.png[] to get the party started.

Click image:pause.png[title=Pause] when you need a break.

// kbmemo:syntax-ref:images-positioning
image:sunset.jpg[Sunset,150,150,role=right] What a beautiful sunset!

// kbmemo:syntax-ref:images-data-uri
:data-uri:

// === Audio / Video ===

// kbmemo:syntax-ref:audio
audio::ocean-waves.wav[]

audio::ocean-waves.wav[start=60,opts=autoplay]

// kbmemo:syntax-ref:video
video::video-file.mp4[]

video::video-file.mp4[width=640,start=60,opts=autoplay]

video::RvRhUHTV_8k[youtube]

video::67480300[vimeo]

// === Keyboard / Menu / Button macros ===

// kbmemo:syntax-ref:macros-kbd-menu-btn
|===
|Shortcut |Purpose

|kbd:[F11]
|Toggle fullscreen

|kbd:[Ctrl+T]
|Open a new tab
|===

To save the file, select menu:File[Save].

Select menu:View[Zoom > Reset] to reset the zoom level to the default setting.

Press the btn:[OK] button when you are finished.

Select a file in the file navigator and click btn:[Open].

// === Literals and source code ===

// kbmemo:syntax-ref:literals-inline
Output literal monospace text, such as `+{backtick}+` or `+http://localhost:8080+`, by enclosing the text in a pair of pluses surrounded by a pair of backticks.

// kbmemo:syntax-ref:literals-paragraph
Normal line.

 Indent line by one space to create a literal line.

Normal line.

// kbmemo:syntax-ref:literals-block
....
error: 1954 Forbidden search
absolutely fatal: operation lost in the dodecahedron of doom

Would you like to try again? y/n
....

// kbmemo:syntax-ref:listing
.Gemfile.lock
----
GEM
  remote: https://rubygems.org/
  specs:
    asciidoctor (2.0.15)

PLATFORMS
  ruby

DEPENDENCIES
  asciidoctor (~> 2.0.15)
----

// kbmemo:syntax-ref:source-highlight
.Some Ruby code
[source,ruby]
----
require 'sinatra'

get '/hi' do
  "Hello World!"
end
----

// kbmemo:syntax-ref:source-callouts
[source,ruby]
----
require 'sinatra' // <1>

get '/hi' do // <2>
  "Hello World!" // <3>
end
----
<1> Library import
<2> URL mapping
<3> HTTP response body

// kbmemo:syntax-ref:source-include
[,ruby]
----
include::app.rb[]
----

// kbmemo:syntax-ref:source-paragraph
[source,xml]
<meta name="viewport"
  content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">

This is normal content.

// === Admonitions ===

// kbmemo:syntax-ref:admonitions-paragraph
NOTE: An admonition draws the reader's attention to auxiliary information.

IMPORTANT: Don't forget the children!

TIP: Look for the warp zone under the bridge.

CAUTION: Slippery when wet.

WARNING: The software you're about to use is untested.

// kbmemo:syntax-ref:admonitions-block
[NOTE]
====
An admonition block may contain compound content.

.A list
- one
- two
- three

Another paragraph.
====

// === More delimited blocks ===

// kbmemo:syntax-ref:blocks-sidebar
.Optional Title
****
Sidebars are used to visually separate auxiliary bits of content
that supplement the main text.
****

// kbmemo:syntax-ref:blocks-example
====
Here's a sample AsciiDoc document:

----
= Title of Document
Doc Writer
:toc:

This guide provides...
----

The document header is useful, but not required.
====

// kbmemo:syntax-ref:blocks-blockquote
[quote,Abraham Lincoln,Address delivered at the dedication of the Cemetery at Gettysburg]
____
Four score and seven years ago our fathers brought forth
on this continent a new nation...
____

[quote,Albert Einstein]
A person who never made a mistake never tried anything new.

____
A person who never made a mistake never tried anything new.
____

// kbmemo:syntax-ref:blocks-open
--
An open block can be an anonymous container,
or it can masquerade as any other block.
--

[source]
--
puts "I'm a source block!"
--

// kbmemo:syntax-ref:blocks-passthrough
++++
<p>
Content in a passthrough block is passed to the output unprocessed.
</p>
++++

// kbmemo:syntax-ref:blocks-subs
:release-version: 2.4.3

[source,xml,subs=attributes+]
----
<dependency>
  <groupId>org.asciidoctor</groupId>
  <artifactId>asciidoctorj</artifactId>
  <version>{release-version}</version>
</dependency>
----

// === Tables ===

// kbmemo:syntax-ref:tables-basic
.Table Title
|===
|Column 1, Header Row |Column 2, Header Row

|Cell in column 1, row 1
|Cell in column 2, row 1

|Cell in column 1, row 2
|Cell in column 2, row 2
|===

// kbmemo:syntax-ref:tables-header-cols
[%header,cols=2*]
|===
|Name of Column 1
|Name of Column 2

|Cell in column 1, row 1
|Cell in column 2, row 1

|Cell in column 1, row 2
|Cell in column 2, row 2
|===

// kbmemo:syntax-ref:tables-asciidoc-cells
[cols="2,2,5a"]
|===
|Firefox
|Browser
|Mozilla Firefox is an open source web browser.

It's designed for:

* standards compliance
* performance
* portability

https://getfirefox.com[Get Firefox]!
|===

// kbmemo:syntax-ref:tables-csv
,===
Artist,Track,Genre

Baauer,Harlem Shake,Hip Hop
,===

[%header,format=csv]
|===
Artist,Track,Genre
Baauer,Harlem Shake,Hip Hop
The Lumineers,Ho Hey,Folk Rock
|===

// kbmemo:syntax-ref:tables-dsv
:===
Artist:Track:Genre

Robyn:Indestructible:Dance
:===

// kbmemo:syntax-ref:tables-formatted
[cols="e,m,^,>s",width="25%"]
|===
|1 >s|2 |3 |4
^|5 2.2+^.^|6 .3+<.>m|7
^|8
|9 2+>|10
|===

// === IDs, roles, options, comments ===

// kbmemo:syntax-ref:ids-roles-options
[#goals.incremental]
* Goal 1
* Goal 2

[id="goals-formal",role="incremental"]
* Goal 1 (formal)
* Goal 2 (formal)

[#null-values]
== Primitive types and null values

[#id-name.role-name]`monospace text`

[#free-world.goals]*free the world*

[%header%footer%autowidth]
|===
|Header A |Header B
|Footer A |Footer B
|===

// kbmemo:syntax-ref:comments
// A single-line comment

////
A multi-line comment.

Notice it's a delimited block.
////

// === Breaks ===

// kbmemo:syntax-ref:breaks-thematic
before

'''

after

// kbmemo:syntax-ref:breaks-page
<<<

// === Attributes and substitutions ===

// kbmemo:syntax-ref:attributes
:url-home: https://asciidoctor.org
:link-docs: https://asciidoctor.org/docs[documentation]
:summary: AsciiDoc is a mature, plain-text document format.
:checkedbox: pass:normal[{startsb}&#10004;{endsb}]

Check out {url-home}[Asciidoctor]!

{summary}

Be sure to read the {link-docs} too!

{checkedbox} That's done!

// kbmemo:syntax-ref:attributes-counter
.Parts{counter2:index:0}
|===
|Part Id |Description

|PX-{counter:index}
|Description of PX-{index}

|PX-{counter:index}
|Description of PX-{index}
|===

// kbmemo:syntax-ref:text-replacements
(C) (R) (TM) -- ... -> => <- <= Sam's

// kbmemo:syntax-ref:escaping
In /items/\{id}, the id attribute isn't replaced.

\*Stars* isn't displayed as bold text.

\https://example.org isn't converted into an active link.

// kbmemo:syntax-ref:passthrough-inline
A word or phrase between single pluses, such as +/user/{id}+,
is not substituted.

+++<del>strike this</del>+++ is marked as deleted.

pass:[<del>strike this</del>] is also marked as deleted.

// === Bibliography ===

// kbmemo:syntax-ref:bibliography
_The Pragmatic Programmer_ <<pp>> should be required reading for all developers.

[bibliography]
== References

* [[[pp]]] Andy Hunt & Dave Thomas. The Pragmatic Programmer. Addison-Wesley. 1999.

// === Footnotes ===

// kbmemo:syntax-ref:footnotes
A statement.footnote:[Clarification about this statement.]

A bold statement!footnote:disclaimer[Opinions are my own.]

Another bold statement.footnote:disclaimer[]

// === Markdown compatibility ===

// kbmemo:syntax-ref:markdown-headings
## Markdown-style Level 2

### Markdown-style Level 3

// kbmemo:syntax-ref:markdown-fenced
```ruby
require 'sinatra'

get '/hi' do
  "Hello World!"
end
```

// kbmemo:syntax-ref:markdown-blockquote
> I hold it that a little rebellion now and then is a good thing,
> and as necessary in the political world as storms in the physical.
> -- Thomas Jefferson, Papers of Thomas Jefferson: Volume 11

// kbmemo:syntax-ref:markdown-thematic
---

- - -

***

* * *

// === KBMemo extensions (not in quick reference) ===

// kbmemo:syntax-ref:kbmemo-wiki
Wiki link: [[syntax-fixture-demo]]

AsciiDoc link: link:/memos/1[Memo one]

// kbmemo:syntax-ref:kbmemo-diagram
diagram::sample.mmd[]

// kbmemo:syntax-ref:kbmemo-math
Inline STEM: stem:[E=mc^2]

Inline LaTeX: latexmath:[\int_0^1 x^2 dx]

[stem]
++++
\sum_{i=1}^{n} i = \frac{n(n+1)}{2}
++++
